summary: Major open-source agent harness (Grok Build), first OpenAI hardware (Codex Micro), Claude-1Password integration, security updates from OpenAI and Anthropic. User reports highlight vibe-coding workflows and context management challenges. Infrastructure tools for eval, security, and runtime continue to emerge. | JetBrains, Mistral, Google, Cloudflare, Anthropic, and Supabase release agentic features; Anthropic shares Claude Code story and containment; Daytona and Modal advance sandbox infrastructure. | Mainstream agent platform releases dominate: Google Gemini CLI, Anthropic Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Microsoft Agent Framework Go, Vercel AI, Langfuse, PydanticAI, Qwen Code, and others ship frequent updates. Infra signals include microVM sandboxing, eval frameworks, and control planes. User workflow signals are absent in this github-only shard. | Package ecosystem shows strong activity in MCP tooling, agent observability, and security scanning. Key signals include a new agent observability package with record/replay, a credential broker for agents, and multiple MCP servers for various platforms. User workflow signals are limited from package sources alone, with most items being infra primitives or new releases. Mainstream product signals are absent from this shard, which focuses on packages rather than vendor changelogs. Gaps include missing user workflow reports and mainstream product updates.
